intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard benefits from simple, clean data visualization with thin strokes and whitespace over dense information density
facade.label: Minimalist line graphs
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity and breathing room; probe toward sparse layouts, monochromatic accents, and icon-driven navigation
facade.rejectImplies: User wants richness and detail; probe toward textured backgrounds, layered information, and colorful data visualization

evidence.hypothesis: A health dashboard benefits from simple, clean data visualization with thin strokes and whitespace over dense information density
evidence.implication: User values clarity and breathing room; probe toward sparse layouts, monochromatic accents, and icon-driven navigation

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for navigation paired with card-based layouts will feel organized yet spacious for tracking multiple health experiments simultaneously
facade.axisTargeted: Information architecture: sidebar vs tabs + card-based vs list-based layout
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers persistent context (sidebar) + modular content blocks (cards) — likely values quick switching between experiments and visual hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either flat tab navigation or continuous scrolling lists — likely values simpler hierarchy or denser information density

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased animations between dashboard views over instant state changes, especially when navigating between experiment timelines.
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Build with CSS transitions and spring animations for view changes; consider staggered micro-animations when loading experiment cards.
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize instant renders and snappy state updates; animations may feel sluggish for frequent data checks; focus on zero-latency interactions.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer small, scannable stat cards over full-page charts for quick health check-ins
facade.axisTargeted: density vs. scannability — how much data per visual unit
facade.label: Daily metric cards
facade.acceptImplies: Build with card-based layout; metric-first design; glanceable summaries over deep dives
facade.rejectImplies: Explore dense dashboard layouts; comprehensive charts occupying full width; narrative summaries over isolated numbers

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, chat-like voice over clinical medical language when tracking personal health experiments
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design copy with casual language, encouraging phrases, and a supportive buddy-like personality throughout the dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: Design copy that is more formal, data-focused, and objective—removing colloquialisms and adopting medical/scientific terminology

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers smooth, gentle transitions in visual hierarchy—suggesting a calm, organic aesthetic for their health dashboard rather than harsh contrasts or clinical rigidity.
facade.label: Soft gradients
facade.acceptImplies: They value visual warmth and fluidity; the dashboard should feel like a living document, not a cold instrument. Color and spacing should breathe.
facade.rejectImplies: They may prefer clarity through bold contrast, structured grids, or a more clinical/scientific visual language—cooler and more intentional in its geometry.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations that encourage reflection

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations that encourage reflection
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard tracking intimate biometric data may feel less intrusive and easier on the eyes with dark mode, creating a sense of privacy and evening-friendly usability
facade.label: Dark backgrounds, light text
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers low-light interfaces, values eye comfort and privacy-conscious aesthetics; probe further into dark palettes, high contrast, and nocturnal use patterns
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers bright, energetic, daytime-oriented interfaces; move toward light backgrounds, natural tones, and high visibility as default
